diagnosis: The player spent ~55 seconds completely stationary at spawn (x=176, y=562) before moving right, indicating no readable path or immediate affordance. The level is a 120+ tile wide horizontal corridor with a repeating ladder-and-platform macro-structure but no mid-level checkpoints, elevation changes, or structural breaks. The exit sits at the far right on the same baseline as spawn, yet the player never reached it — they turned back around x=3830 after ~97s, then stalled again at x=3482 for the final ~20s. Seven enemies were killed and two hits taken, proving combat occurs but on a flat plane that lesson_0003 already identifies as low-engagement. The comment confirms the exit path was not perceivable.

lessons.lesson: A spawn on a featureless floor with no visible exit, platform, or directional cue causes extended idle time before the first meaningful input, even when the level is technically traversable.
conditions: spawn on flat floor with no adjacent platforms or gaps
exit far away and not visible from spawn
no arrows, lighting, or terrain hints toward critical path
lessons.outcome: player idled for 55 seconds at start, engagement dropped before gameplay began
